pbq.title: Windows application support tickets
pbq.scenario: A help desk technician is triaging three Windows tickets that started after routine updates.

items.label: Ticket A
items.value: Line-of-business app crashes only for one user profile

items.label: Ticket B
items.value: Laptop boots to recovery after repeated failed startup attempts

items.value: Browser redirects search results and shows unexpected extensions

items.label: Ticket A
items.value: Same app works under a newly created local test account

items.label: Ticket B
items.value: Startup Repair is available from Windows Recovery Environment

items.label: Ticket C
items.value: Endpoint protection shows a potentially unwanted program alert

pbq.task: Use profile, recovery, and endpoint evidence to choose the safest next software-repair action.

question: Match each support ticket to the best next action.
itemsLeft: Application crashes only inside one damaged user profile
System repeatedly fails startup and enters recovery
Browser redirects and unwanted extensions appear after a PUP alert
itemsRight: Run antimalware cleanup, remove suspicious extensions, and reset browser settings
Use WinRE Startup Repair or restore options before reinstalling the OS
Repair the user profile or migrate data to a new profile after confirming app health
explanation: A profile-only crash should be handled as profile corruption after confirming the app works elsewhere. Repeated boot failure belongs in WinRE repair workflow. Redirects with unwanted extensions and a PUP alert call for malware/PUP cleanup and browser reset.

componentFeedback.label: Application crashes only inside one damaged user profile
componentFeedback.action: Repair the user profile or migrate data to a new profile after confirming app health
componentFeedback.why: The action isolates the smallest likely software, profile, service, credential, or capacity fault before broader recovery.

componentFeedback.label: System repeatedly fails startup and enters recovery
componentFeedback.action: Use WinRE Startup Repair or restore options before reinstalling the OS
componentFeedback.why: The action isolates the smallest likely software, profile, service, credential, or capacity fault before broader recovery.

componentFeedback.label: Browser redirects and unwanted extensions appear after a PUP alert
componentFeedback.action: Run antimalware cleanup, remove suspicious extensions, and reset browser settings
componentFeedback.why: The action isolates the smallest likely software, profile, service, credential, or capacity fault before broader recovery.
